reviews.review_msg: Great Prof. Very interactive class. Forces you to pay attention. Loves what he does. One of the best business profs at SFU.
Very good professor, really enjoys his job and his students.  Prints out the notes for the class - bonus.  Very helpful when you need help and material is easy - great prof

One of the best profs I've had at SFU, winner of the TD Bank Distinguished Teaching Award. He is very knowledgeable in HR, has a very high interest in course content, and even helps place students in jobs after graduation. Lectures are fairly interesting & interactive.

He will make a judgment about you right away. Go to his office and make sure that he thinks you are a keener because the material is so subjective his grades are often based on what he thinks about you. If you can take something else instead do it, it wasn't worth the money.

Just an excellent teacher.  Very easy to talk to, and will go out of his way to help any student.  Class material is quite applicable to the HR industry.  Another note on Rick, he is helping a group of student get ready for an HR case competition in Montreal. How many Profs go that far for students?

Rick is the best, he motivates and keeps everyone interested and is very helpful and supportive. He even gives out his home number for extra help and planned an end of the semester pub lunch for the entire class to celebrate the end of the class. How many profs do that?

I don't usually rate my professors but Natalie is by far my favourite professor at SFU!! This is my second time taking a course with her and she is the absolute best. She is the most caring, approachable and sweetest human ever. I HIGHLY RECOMMEND

I enjoyed this class. Very little of the class was spent just listening to lecture. Lots of discussions and activities. Groups are formed and they present a chapter and case study 1 week in the semester. After each presentation Natalie ensures all important concepts are covered. Natalie is friendly, good at explaining concepts. Highly recommend.

Exceptional professor in SFU. She really shows her care to the class. Natalie is the professor that will always go above and beyond than just read the book off and give grades, she will instead bring up useful materials and news in current HR world and let the students be updated. If you are in HR, make sure you have a class with her!

Great prof and lectures are always interesting. Get ready to read and discuss case studies every week! Nearing the end of the semester, you will be preparing recommendations to help a real company mitigate/solve its issues.
